title: Basic MongoDB CRUD Operations
description: Demonstrates fundamental database operations in MongoDB shell
explanation: CRUD operations form the foundation of MongoDB interactions. insertOne/addMany create documents, find retrieves them, updateOne/updateMany modify them, and delete removes them.

complexity.time: O(log n) with index, O(n) without

title: Aggregation Pipeline Example
description: Shows data analysis using aggregation framework
explanation: Aggregation pipeline processes documents through multiple stages. $match filters data, $unwind expands arrays, $group aggregates data, $sort orders results, and $project reshapes output.

complexity.time: O(n) at each stage, overall O(n)

title: Index Creation and Query Optimization
description: Demonstrates creating indexes and analyzing queries
explanation: Indexes significantly improve query performance. Unique indexes ensure data integrity, compound indexes optimize multi-field queries, and text indexes enable full-text search.

complexity.time: Index creation: O(n log n), Queries: O(log n) with index

title: MongoDB Data Modeling Examples
description: Shows embedded vs referenced document patterns
explanation: Embedded documents store related data together for fast reads. Referenced documents use IDs for normalization and are better for frequently updated data. $lookup performs joins between collections.

complexity.time: Embedded: O(1) read, Referenced: O(n) with lookup

title: Replica Set Configuration
description: Demonstrates setting up and using replica sets
explanation: Replica sets provide high availability with automatic failover. Write concern ensures write acknowledgment, and read preference distributes queries to secondaries for load balancing.

complexity.time: Replication is asynchronous, O(1) for failover
